briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposed AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protections in embedded banking.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment network in 2026, signalling a major shift away from Visa and Mastercard dominance in Europe.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a new wave of unconventional credit card products with novel rewards, structures, and underwriting models that challenge traditional banking norms.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape and what that means for consumers and financial institutions alike.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Saris raises $28.8-million USD Series A to automate banks' back offices](https://betakit.com/saris-raises-28-8-million-usd-series-a-to-automate-banks-back-offices/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech Saris has closed a $28.8M USD Series A round to scale its AI-powered back-office automation platform for financial institutions.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada officially labelled screen scraping as insecure and laid out an open banking roadmap, a foundational moment still shaping the country's consumer-directed finance journey today.

- **[Bank of Canada Says Markets More Vulnerable to Sharp Correction](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/bank-of-canada-says-markets-more-vulnerable-to-sharp-correction)** — *Financial Post* — The Bank of Canada warned that financial markets face elevated risk of a sharp asset price correction, while noting the financial system has so far weathered recent global shocks.

- **[Canadians increasingly struggle to keep up with mortgage payments, report says](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/mortgage-delinquencies-up-9.7212533?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — A new report reveals rising mortgage delinquency rates across Canada, highlighting growing financial stress among homeowners and potential implications for lenders and credit unions.

- **[Canada has a $500-billion opportunity if it acts more like the U.S., Balsillie says](https://financialpost.com/technology/canada-500-billion-opportunity-us-balsillie)** — *Financial Post* — Jim Balsillie argues Canada is stuck in an outdated economic model and must modernize its policy framework to capture a $500-billion opportunity in the digital and innovation economy.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Upset Uber customers charged for monthly memberships they say they never signed up for](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/uber-one-membership-charges-9.7212871?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Complaints from Canadian consumers about unauthorized Uber One subscription charges underscore the need for stronger embedded finance consumer protections and clearer consent frameworks — a key issue for Atlantic Canada's financial inclusion agenda.

- **[Investor burn books and FaceTiming Serena Williams: three founder fundraising stories from Homecoming](https://betakit.com/from-an-investor-burn-book-to-facetiming-serena-williams-three-founders-share-their-fundraising-stories/)** — *BetaKit* — Founders share candid fundraising experiences from Collision's Homecoming event, offering practical insights relevant to Atlantic Canadian fintech startups navigating early-stage venture capital.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking credit startup Credit Kudos signals Big Tech's growing interest in financial data and alternative credit scoring — a trend with direct implications for Canadian open banking policy and Atlantic fintech builders.
